mission.title: Install and Configure Cluster API (CAPI)
mission.description: Cluster API (CAPI) is a Kubernetes operator that provides declarative APIs and tooling to simplify the provisioning, upgrading, and management of multiple Kubernetes clusters. It is particularly useful for platform operators looking to automate cluster lifecycle management across various infrastructure environments. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and the need for familiarity with Kubernetes concepts.

steps.title: Add the Cluster API Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Cluster API Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add cluster-api https://charts.cluster-api.sigs.k8s.io

```

> This is the official repository for Cluster API.

steps.title: Install the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs)
steps.description:
CRDs are necessary for Cluster API to function as they define the custom resources used by the operator.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api-components.yaml

```

> This command installs the required CRDs.

steps.title: Install Cluster API components
steps.description:
This step installs the Cluster API components in the specified namespace.

```bash

helm install cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace kube-system --version v1.12.4

```

> Ensure you have the kube-system namespace created.
commands: helm install cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace kube-system --version v1.12.4

steps.title: Create a Cluster API custom resource
steps.description:
This step creates a Cluster API custom resource to manage your Kubernetes clusters.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
    kind: MyInfrastructureProvider
    name: my-infra
EOF

```

> Replace 'MyInfrastructureProvider' with your actual infrastructure provider.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
    kind: MyInfrastructureProvider
    name: my-infra
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Cluster API installation
steps.description:
This step checks if the Cluster API components are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kube-system

```

> You should see the Cluster API components running.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Cluster API components
uninstall.description:
This will remove the Cluster API components and CRDs. Be aware that this may lead to data loss if clusters are managed by CAPI.

```bash

helm uninstall cluster-api --namespace kube-system

kubectl delete -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api-components.yaml

```

> This removes the CRDs.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Cluster API components
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your resources. This step will upgrade the Cluster API components to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get cluster -o yaml > cluster-backup.yaml

helm upgrade cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace kube-system --version v1.12.4

kubectl get pods -n kube-system

```

> Backup your cluster resources.
> This command upgrades the Cluster API components.
> Verify that the pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: helm upgrade cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace kube-system --version v1.12.4

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no matches for kind "Cluster" in version "cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the CRDs for Cluster API have not been installed correctly.

**Fix:**
Reapply the CRDs using the command: kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api-components.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create cluster: infrastructure provider not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This suggests that the specified infrastructure provider CRD is not installed or misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the correct infrastructure provider CRDs are installed and configured properly.

troubleshooting.title: Pods in kube-system are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can occur due to misconfiguration in the Cluster API components.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the pods using: kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kube-system and fix any configuration issues.

troubleshooting.title: kubectl get clusters returns no resources found
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that no Cluster API custom resources have been created.

**Fix:**
Create a Cluster API custom resource using the provided YAML template.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new infrastructure providers and improved the stability of existing components.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the old API versions for infrastructure providers.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.11.x, ensure to update your custom resources to the new API versions.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods -n kube-system' showing all Cluster API components in the Running state.
codeSnippets: helm install cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace kube-system --version v1.12.4

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
    kind: MyInfrastructureProvider
    name: my-infra
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the required resources.
